channel.title: Multilingual - source url + match (WPML)
channel.description:
EDIT:
My original question was regarding setting the state source dynamically. I have since updated to use `match` (multiple domains).
**Internal Vs External links when using `match`**
https://community.frontity.org/t/multilingual-update-source-url-from-state-wpml/4962/7?u=tim.arney
ORIGINAL Question :
Hi
Wondering if there's a way to set `@frontity/wp-source` url dynamically based on state or get the router to add "/fr" (language) to the url if a language is set.
Trying to use Heroku (single domain)
i.e. I'm setting up a language toggle and would like to fetch from `https://my-url` (english) or `https://my-url/fr` (french) ``` packages: [ { name: "@frontity/wp-source", state: { source: { url: "https://my-url", // add fr to this as needed from theme state homepage: "/home", }, }, }, ```
I see I can do something like this using match based on the domain / url
Was thinking I might be able to do something with match but not sure how I can add setup the router to use /fr/my-page when I want to use my french rest endpoint

item.title: Multilingual - source url + match (WPML)
item.dc:creator: tim.arney
item.description:
For now I patched the package on my end which solves the issue given my use case

github.com

cds-snc/platform-mvp/blob/main/cds-snc-docs/patches/@frontity+components+1.8.0.patch

diff --git a/node_modules/@frontity/components/.DS_Store b/node_modules/@frontity/components/.DS_Store
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..da24698
Binary files /dev/null and b/node_modules/@frontity/components/.DS_Store differ
diff --git a/node_modules/@frontity/components/link/utils.ts b/node_modules/@frontity/components/link/utils.ts
index 894784b..f7efd96 100644
--- a/node_modules/@frontity/components/link/utils.ts
+++ b/node_modules/@frontity/components/link/utils.ts
@@ -129,9 +129,11 @@ export const removeSourceUrl = ({
   match,
 }: RemoveSourceUrlParams) => {
   // if match is present we need to ensure the internal link matches the current site url pattern
+  /*
   if (match && !match.some((regexp) => new RegExp(regexp).test(link))) {
     return link;
   }
+  */
 
   // Ensure `sourceUrl` and `frontityUrl` always include a trailing slash. All
   // the logic below is based on those variables fulfilling that condition.

Read full topic

item.title: Multilingual - source url + match (WPML)
item.dc:creator: tim.arney
item.description:
Thinking this comes down to hosting the front-end on a domain that doesn’t match the WP REST endpoints

regexp .*https?://my-site.herokuapp.com

The REST endpoint
link https://my-wp-install.com/fr/my-page/

if (match && !match.some((regexp) => new RegExp(regexp).test(link))) {
    return link;
}

Read full topic

item.title: Multilingual - source url + match (WPML)
item.dc:creator: tim.arney
item.description:
@David

I was looking at

github.com/frontity/frontity

Fix `Link` component for multisites

frontity:dev ← frontity:fix-link-component-wp-subdir
opened 04:20PM - 16 Apr 21 UTC
DAreRodz
+239 -67

**What**: Fixes #691 **Why**: Right now, the `Link` component doesn't r…emove the subirectory of a WordPress multisite (e.g. `/wp-site` in `http://backend.url/wp-site/some-post`), making links in Frontity navigate to the wrong URL. **How**: Fixing the `removeSourceUrl` function (WIP): https://github.com/frontity/frontity/blob/f0b6deedef03b2a713c52cbd88e379eb26d8f333/packages/components/link/utils.ts#L117-L141 **Tasks**: - [x] Code - [x] TSDocs - [x] Unit tests - [x] Add a changeset (with link to its [Feature Discussion](https://community.frontity.org/c/33) if it exists) **Unrelated Tasks** - [ ] TypeScript - [ ] End to end tests - [ ] TypeScript tests - [ ] Update starter themes - [ ] Update other packages - [ ] Update community discussions

And wondering if I’ve found a bug or misconfigured something

When I try to use match all links end up being external

If I comment out this code the pages load correctly

if (match && !match.some((regexp) => new RegExp(regexp).test(link))) {
    return link;
}

Currently testing using match + localhost

github.com

cds-snc/platform-mvp/blob/main/cds-snc-docs/frontity.settings.js

const settings = [
  {
    name: "covid-alert",
    match: [
      ".*https?://localhost:3000",
      ".*https?://platform-covid-alert.herokuapp.com",
    ],
    state: {
      frontity: {
        title: "-",
        description: "-",
        url: "https://localhost:3000",
      },
    },
    packages: [
      {
        name: "@frontity/wp-source",
        state: {
          source: {
            url: "https://platform.digital.canada.ca/covid-alert/fr",
This file has been truncated. show original

Read full topic

item.title: Multilingual - source url + match (WPML)
item.dc:creator: tim.arney
item.description:
Maybe this is related to:

Looks like the issue is related to

if (match && !match.some((regexp) => new RegExp(regexp).test(link))) {
    return link;
}
github.com

frontity/frontity/blob/dev/packages/components/link/utils.ts#L132

    
      
    
          
  1. *
    2. 
          
  2. * @returns The URL without the Source URL.
    3. 
          
  3. */
    4. 
          
  4. export const removeSourceUrl = ({
    5. 
          
  5.  link,
    6. 
          
  6.  sourceUrl,
    7. 
          
  7.  frontityUrl = "/",
    8. 
          
  8.  match,
    9. 
          
  9. }: RemoveSourceUrlParams) => {
    10. 
          
  10.  // if match is present we need to ensure the internal link matches the current site url pattern
    11. 
          
  11.  if (match && !match.some((regexp) => new RegExp(regexp).test(link))) {
    12. 
          
  12.    return link;
    13. 
          
  13.  }
    14. 
          
  14. 
          
    15. 

  15.  // Ensure `sourceUrl` and `frontityUrl` always include a trailing slash. All
    16. 
          
  16.  // the logic below is based on those variables fulfilling that condition.
    17. 
          
  17.  sourceUrl = sourceUrl.replace(/\/?$/, "/");
    18. 
          
  18.  frontityUrl = frontityUrl.replace(/\/?$/, "/");
    19. 
          
  19. 
          
    20. 

  20.  const { host: sourceHost, pathname: sourcePath } = new URL(sourceUrl);
    21. 
          
  21.  const { pathname: frontityPath } = new URL(frontityUrl, sourceUrl);
    22.

Summary so far:
With no match set - my sidebar links load the correct pages and content i.e. internal links — but of course this doesn’t work for multilingual
With match set - my sidebar links end up as external links

I opened up the repo I’m playing around with

github.com

cds-snc/platform-mvp/blob/main/cds-snc-docs/frontity.settings.js#L4

    
      
    
          
  1. const settings = [
    2. 
          
  2.   {
    3. 
          
  3.     name: "covid-alert",
    4. 
          
  4.     match: [
    5. 
          
  5.       ".*https?://localhost:3000",
    6. 
          
  6.       ".*https?://platform-covid-alert.herokuapp.com",
    7. 
          
  7.     ],
    8. 
          
  8.     state: {
    9. 
          
  9.       frontity: {
    10. 
          
  10.         title: "-",
    11. 
          
  11.         description: "-",
    12. 
          
  12.       },
    13. 
          
  13.     },
    14. 
          
  14.     packages: [
    15.

Read full topic

item.title: Multilingual - source url + match (WPML)
item.dc:creator: kasper
item.description:
Hi @Johan and @tim.arney,

There is actually a working example:

https://www.texasmattressmakers.com/

Thee only problem is that they don’t have a public repo. From what I can gather, it looks like they are using embedded mode, since the /wp-json is accessible directly on the main domain:

https://www.texasmattressmakers.com/wp-json/

I also can’t see a namespace for any multi language plugins… Maybe they are doing translations on the react side, with some kind of npm package. Because even when I have the language set to spanish, the rest api language is english.

Read full topic

item.title: Multilingual - source url + match (WPML)
item.dc:creator: tim.arney
item.description:
Hmm

When I use match

match: [".*https?:\/\/localhost:4000"],

my urls end up linking to my “remote” rest api (the one I set for source) - this breaks the links as they link out - to the actual WP website.

name: "@frontity/wp-source",
        state: {
          source: {
            url: "https://my-url.com/fr",
            homepage: "/home-fr",
          },
        },

When I don’t use match things are working fine for the links - the links go to the right pages in french.

Read full topic

item.title: Multilingual - source url + match (WPML)
item.dc:creator: tim.arney
item.description:
Thanks Johan

I’ll probably end up going down the multiple domains + match setup

Read full topic

item.title: Multilingual - source url + match (WPML)
item.dc:creator: Johan
item.description:
Multilingual Frontity sites are not really supported and I haven’t seen a working solution yet.

I did some testing (see: Multilanguage with WPML - #2 by Johan), including changing the URL or parameters (most plugins support ?lang=xx) but without any result.

The only (sort of) working solution I found is to make 2 different sites within a single Frontity setup which changes based on the URL, however switching languages will not work properly with this.

Read full topic

item.title: Multilingual - source url + match (WPML)
item.dc:creator: tim.arney
item.description:
EDIT:

My original question was regarding setting the state source dynamically. I have since updated to use match (multiple domains).

Internal Vs External links when using match

Multilingual - source url + match (WPML) 🙋Get Help
Maybe this is related to: Summary so far: With no match set - my sidebar links load the correct pages and content i.e. internal links — but of course this doesn’t work for multilingual With match set - my sidebar links end up as external links I opened up the repo I’m playing around with

ORIGINAL Question :

Hi

Wondering if there’s a way to set @frontity/wp-source url dynamically based on state or get the router to add “/fr” (language) to the url if a language is set.

Trying to use Heroku (single domain)

i.e. I’m setting up a language toggle and would like to fetch from https://my-url (english) or https://my-url/fr (french)

 packages: [
      {
        name: "@frontity/wp-source",
        state: {
          source: {
            url: "https://my-url", // add fr to this as needed from theme state
            homepage: "/home",
          },
        },
      },

I see I can do something like this using match based on the domain / url

Was thinking I might be able to do something with match but not sure how I can add setup the router to use /fr/my-page when I want to use my french rest endpoint

Read full topic
